title: Tapping for Permission to Sleep
subtitle: A nighttime tapping practice for releasing the sense that sleep must be earned
excerpt: A five-minute nighttime tapping practice from Day 26 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script addresses the pattern of lying awake with the sense that there is more to do, more to justify, more to prove before sleep is allowed. The reframe moves toward sleep as something you already have permission for — not a reward for enough done.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 26 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are in bed but the mind is still running a list of what wasn't done, or when guilt about resting is keeping you awake.

content.text: A five-minute nighttime tapping practice from Day 26 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script targets the not-enough-done pattern: lying down but unable to stop, the mind still running the list of things left. The reframe moves toward the specific felt experience of permission — the exhale of 'done enough, safe to stop'.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I sometimes think I should be doing more, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though stopping feels uncomfortable, I honour the growth it takes to allow rest.
Even though guilt tries to sneak in, I am open to letting permission take its place.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've done enough for today.
Side of eye: The world keeps turning without me.
Under eye: I can let myself be still.
Under nose: I don't need to justify sleep.
Chin: This rest is earned by being human.
Collarbone: My body thanks me for finally stopping.
Under arm: Permission feels like release.
Top of head: I let that release carry me to sleep.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I sleep easily because I am allowed to.
Side of eye: I rest without guilt, fully and freely.
Under eye: Permission relaxes my whole system.
Under nose: Stillness is my strength tonight.
Chin: I am safe to release the day completely.
Collarbone: My rest is well-deserved.
Under arm: I close my eyes knowing I have done enough.
Top of head: I sleep peacefully in total permission.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 26 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
